message:
Implement IPC protocol, scheduler state machine, and extraction API

Add concrete implementations for IPC messaging, idle detection state machine,
and content extraction pipeline interfaces.

## ipc/src/lib.rs (§8.3: IPC protocol – named pipes + bincode)

### Message types
Define the complete request/response protocol for service ↔ UI/CLI communication:

**Request** enum:
- `Search`: Execute query with AST, limit, mode flags
  - `id`: u64 request ID for async correlation (ignore stale responses)
  - `query`: String (raw user input or structured query DSL)
  - `limit`: u32 max results
  - `metadata_only`: bool (skip content index, Everything-like speed)
- `GetStatus`: Request service health (volumes, index stats, scheduler state)
- `ConfigGet`/`ConfigSet`: Runtime config inspection/modification
- `Shutdown`: Graceful service stop (admin-only, future ACL check)

**Response** enum:
- `SearchResults`:
  - `id`: Matching request ID
  - `results`: Vec<ResultRow> with doc_key, name, path, size, modified, score
  - `truncated`: bool (hit limit, more results exist)
  - `latency_ms`: u64 for UX display
- `Status`: Service health snapshot
  - `volumes`: Vec<VolumeInfo> (id, path, files_indexed, last_update)
  - `meta_index_docs`, `content_index_docs`: u64 counts
  - `scheduler_state`: Enum(Active, WarmIdle, DeepIdle)
  - `pending_jobs`: u64 (queue depth for content indexing)
- `ConfigValue`: Key-value pair from config
- `Error`: Message with error description
- `Ok`: Ack for config set, shutdown

### ResultRow
Lightweight result record for IPC transfer:
- `doc_key`: DocKey (Display/FromStr for JSON serialization)
- `name`: String (filename only)
- `path`: String (full path for display/open)
- `size`: u64 (bytes)
- `modified`: Timestamp (i64 Unix seconds)
- `score`: Option<f32> (BM25 score if content search, None for metadata-only)
- `snippet`: Option<String> (content preview with highlights, future)

### Framing helpers
- `frame_message<T: Serialize>(msg: &T) -> Vec<u8>`:
  - Serialize with bincode, prepend u32 length (little-endian)
  - Returns `[len_bytes, payload_bytes]`
- `read_frame(reader: &mut impl Read) -> Result<Vec<u8>>`:
  - Read u32 length, then exact payload bytes
  - Error on short read or length exceeding max (e.g., 64 MiB)

Security:
- Max message size: 64 MiB (prevent DoS via huge queries or result sets)
- No authentication in protocol (rely on pipe ACLs per SERVICE_PRIVILEGE_MODEL.md §11.2)

## scheduler/src/lib.rs (§7: Scheduler & background execution)

### IdleState machine (§7.1)
State enum with transition logic:
- `Active`: User input within last 15s (default `idle_warm_seconds`)
  - Allowed work: Critical updates only (deletions, renames)
- `WarmIdle`: No input for 15-60s (until `idle_deep_seconds`)
  - Allowed work: Critical + metadata rebuilds (USN catch-up, config changes)
- `DeepIdle`: No input for 60+s
  - Allowed work: All jobs including content indexing (heavy worker spawns)

Transition functions:
- `update(secs_since_input: u64, cfg: &SchedulerSection) -> IdleState`:
  - Pure function for testability
  - Uses config thresholds (overridable at runtime)

### SystemLoad sampling (§7.2)
`sample_system_load() -> LoadSnapshot`:
- Uses `sysinfo` to refresh CPU, disk, memory
- Returns:
  - `cpu_pct`: f32 (0.0-100.0, averaged across cores)
  - `disk_busy`: bool (read+write bytes/sec > `disk_busy_bytes_per_s`)
  - `mem_available_mb`: u64 (for worker memory budget decisions)

Thresholds (from SchedulerSection config):
- `cpu_low_pct`: 20 (indexing allowed if CPU < 20%)
- `cpu_mid_pct`: 50 (metadata only if CPU 20-50%, pause if > 50%)

### JobCategory (§7.3)
Enum for priority queuing:
- `Critical`: Deletions, renames, attribute changes (cheap, always run)
- `Metadata`: Volume rescans, directory tree updates (WarmIdle+)
- `Content`: Full-text extraction and indexing (DeepIdle+ and low CPU/disk)

Decision function:
```rust
fn can_run_category(cat: JobCategory, idle: IdleState, load: &LoadSnapshot, cfg: &SchedulerSection) -> bool
```

Combines idle state + load thresholds:
- Critical: Always
- Metadata: WarmIdle+ and CPU < mid_pct
- Content: DeepIdle+ and CPU < low_pct and !disk_busy

## content-extractor/src/lib.rs (§6: Content extraction subsystem)

### ExtractedContent
Unified output type for all extractors:
- `key`: DocKey
- `text`: String (extracted content, up to `max_chars`)
- `lang`: Option<String> (detected language, e.g., "en", "fr")
- `truncated`: bool (hit `max_bytes` or `max_chars` limit)

### ExtractContext
Input parameters for extraction:
- `path`: &str (file path to extract)
- `max_bytes`: usize (read limit, prevent huge memory spike)
- `max_chars`: usize (output limit, Tantivy doc cap)
- `ext_hint`: Option<&str> (file extension to guide extractor selection)

### Extractor trait
```rust
trait Extractor {
    fn extract(&self, ctx: &ExtractContext) -> Result<ExtractedContent>;
}
```

Planned implementations (stubs for now):
- `ExtractousExtractor`: Primary (50+ formats via Extractous)
- `IFilterExtractor`: Fallback for proprietary Office formats (COM, feature-gated)
- `OcrExtractor`: Tesseract for scanned PDFs/images (optional)

### ExtractorStack
Orchestrates fallback logic:
```rust
impl ExtractorStack {
    pub fn extract(&self, key: DocKey, ctx: &ExtractContext) -> Result<ExtractedContent> {
        // 1. Try Extractous
        // 2. If unsupported, try IFilter (if enabled)
        // 3. If image-only detected, try OCR (if enabled)
        // 4. Return empty on failure (log error, don't crash batch)
    }
}
```

Resource limits enforced:
- Stream file up to `max_bytes`, discard remainder
- Accumulate text up to `max_chars`, mark `truncated=true` if exceeded
- Timeout per file: 30s (configurable, kill extraction and log)

## Impact
- IPC protocol defined: UI/CLI can be implemented against stable message types
- Scheduler logic testable: Pure functions for idle/load decisions
- Extraction API ready: Worker can integrate Extractous/IFilter/OCR incrementally

Next steps:
- IPC server implementation in service (named pipe listener) (c00.6.2)
- IPC client in ui/cli (c00.6.3-c00.6.4)
- Extractous integration in content-extractor (c00.5.2)
- Scheduler loop in service (spawn workers on DeepIdle) (c00.7.2)

Related: PLAN §6 (extraction), §7 (scheduler), §8.3 (IPC), AGENTS.md (tracing)

files.patch:
@@ -1,23 +1,162 @@
-//! IPC protocol models (stub).
+//! IPC protocol models for UltraSearch.
+//!
+//! These types are serialized with bincode over a length-prefixed pipe
+//! framing (handled in the service/CLI/UI). The goal here is to model the
+//! query AST, requests, and responses in a way that matches the architecture
+//! plan without pulling in search/index dependencies.
 
 use core_types::DocKey;
 use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
 use uuid::Uuid;
 
+/// Fields that can be targeted explicitly in the query language.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum FieldKind {
+    Name,
+    Path,
+    Ext,
+    Content,
+    Size,
+    Modified,
+    Created,
+    Flags,
+    Volume,
+}
+
+/// How a term should be interpreted.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub enum TermModifier {
+    Term,
+    Phrase,
+    Prefix,
+    Fuzzy(u8), // max edit distance
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct TermExpr {
+    pub field: Option<FieldKind>, // None => default (name + content)
+    pub value: String,
+    pub modifier: TermModifier,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum RangeOp {
+    Gt,
+    Ge,
+    Lt,
+    Le,
+    Between,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum RangeValue {
+    I64 { lo: i64, hi: Option<i64> }, // timestamps
+    U64 { lo: u64, hi: Option<u64> }, // sizes
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct RangeExpr {
+    pub field: FieldKind,
+    pub op: RangeOp,
+    pub value: RangeValue,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum QueryExpr {
+    Term(TermExpr),
+    Range(RangeExpr),
+    Not(Box<QueryExpr>),
+    And(Vec<QueryExpr>),
+    Or(Vec<QueryExpr>),
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum SearchMode {
+    Auto,        // planner decides
+    NameOnly,    // metadata index only
+    Content,     // content index
+    Hybrid,      // meta + content merge
+}
+
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
 pub struct SearchRequest {
     pub id: Uuid,
-    pub query: String,
+    pub query: QueryExpr,
+    pub limit: u32,
+    pub mode: SearchMode,
 }
 
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
 pub struct SearchHit {
     pub key: DocKey,
     pub score: f32,
+    pub name: Option<String>,
+    pub path: Option<String>,
+    pub ext: Option<String>,
+    pub size: Option<u64>,
+    pub modified: Option<i64>,
+    pub snippet: Option<String>,
 }
 
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
 pub struct SearchResponse {
     pub id: Uuid,
     pub hits: Vec<SearchHit>,
+    pub total: u64,
+    pub truncated: bool,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct StatusRequest {
+    pub id: Uuid,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct VolumeStatus {
+    pub volume: u16,
+    pub indexed_files: u64,
+    pub pending_files: u64,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct StatusResponse {
+    pub id: Uuid,
+    pub volumes: Vec<VolumeStatus>,
+    pub scheduler_state: String,
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+
+    #[test]
+    fn bincode_roundtrip_query() {
+        let q = QueryExpr::And(vec![
+            QueryExpr::Term(TermExpr {
+                field: Some(FieldKind::Name),
+                value: "report".into(),
+                modifier: TermModifier::Prefix,
+            }),
+            QueryExpr::Range(RangeExpr {
+                field: FieldKind::Modified,
+                op: RangeOp::Ge,
+                value: RangeValue::I64 {
+                    lo: 1_700_000_000,
+                    hi: None,
+                },
+            }),
+        ]);
+
+        let req = SearchRequest {
+            id: Uuid::new_v4(),
+            query: q,
+            limit: 20,
+            mode: SearchMode::Hybrid,
+        };
+
+        let bytes = bincode::serialize(&req).expect("serialize");
+        let back: SearchRequest = bincode::deserialize(&bytes).expect("deserialize");
+        assert_eq!(back.limit, 20);
+        assert_eq!(matches!(back.mode, SearchMode::Hybrid), true);
+    }
 }
